sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Let me check the current state. I've been going in circles. Let me think about this more carefully.
The cross moves in increments of 4 columns. The gap is at cols 28-29. The cross is 4 columns wide.
Possible cross positions: ..., 10-13, 14-17, 18-21, 22-25, 26-29, 30-33, ...
The gap is at cols 28-29. For the cross to "align" with the gap:
- If the cross is at cols 26-29, the cross's right half (cols 28-29) overlaps with the gap.
- If the cross is at cols 28-31, the cross's left half (cols 28-29) overlaps with the gap.
- If the cross is at cols 30-33, the cross doesn't overlap with the gap.
So the cross can overlap with the gap at cols 26-29 or cols 28-31. But the cross moves in increments of 4 columns, so the cross can be at cols 26-29 but not cols 28-31.
Wait, let me check: the cross is at cols 22-25 now. Clicking the bottom blue block once moves the cross to cols 26-29. Clicking the bottom blue block again would move the cross to cols 30-33 (if the middle white allows it).
But the middle white is gone, so the cross can't move right anymore.
Let me check the current state.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    if n['color'] in ('c', 'N', 'W', 'G', 'b'):
        print(f"color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} bbox=({min(rows)},{min(cols)},{max(rows)},{max(cols)})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
